body.title: CLOSED: instance.proposals queue — controlled write channel for Igor-approved clan.memories entries
body.result: proposals.py + instance.proposals schema with occurrence_count dedup; 9 tests pass (42a44a45)
body.description:
New `instance.proposals` table (psycopg2 direct, same pattern as watch_problems.py). API in `wild_igor/igor/cognition/proposals.py`: add_proposal(kind, content, metadata=None) / read_pending() / commit_proposal(id, memory_id) / reject_proposal(id, reason). Dreaming, librarian, and playbook modules WRITE proposals here; Igor NE habits COMMIT accepted proposals to clan.memories. Enforces the 'Igor decides what goes into clan.memories' architectural principle.

Schema: id serial PK, kind text (habit|watch_q|playbook|archive_action), content text, metadata jsonb default '{}', status text default 'pending' (pending|committed|rejected), source_module text, created_at timestamptz default now(), committed_at timestamptz, committed_memory_id bigint, rejected_at timestamptz, rejected_reason text.

**Affected files:** `wild_igor/igor/cognition/proposals.py` (new), DB migration (CREATE TABLE instance.proposals)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; direct psycopg2 (no cortex.py touch); never hard-delete (rejected proposals stay with rejected_at + reason); status column not boolean — three states needed; source_module records which module submitted the proposal
**Scope boundary:** IN — proposals.py CRUD module, instance.proposals schema, migration. OUT — no automated commit logic (lives in NE habits), no UI, no cross-instance proposals, no cortex.py integration.
**Test plan:** add_proposal() inserts row with status=pending; read_pending() returns only pending rows; commit_proposal() sets committed_at + committed_memory_id; reject_proposal() sets rejected_at + rejected_reason; committed and rejected rows excluded from read_pending()
